title: Use resolved stop mutation identity
goal: Harden the stop idempotency slice by using resolved target identity for live stop mutations while retaining exact-session replay after the member is already removed.
requiredInputs: Potential stale alias replay risk from raw target mutation keys
permissions: Modify swarm stop handler and tests
Run cargo fmt/check/test and selfdev build
completionConditions: Completed stop replay still happens before target resolution when the caller repeats an exact session-id stop after the member has already been removed.
Live stop requests use the resolved target session id for the mutation key, so friendly-name/prefix/suffix aliases do not replay a prior stop if a new current member matches the same alias.
Duplicate concurrent alias stops still share the same resolved-target mutation and do not emit duplicate `SessionCloseRequested` side effects.
Focused stop replay tests, cargo fmt/check, git diff check, and selfdev build pass.
failureTaxonomy: Breaking exact session-id stop replay after member removal
Reintroducing duplicate close requests for concurrent alias retries
Replaying stale stop results for a newly matched friendly name
Changing stop authorization or force behavior
Over-expanding into cleanup or stop protocol redesign
notes: This is a follow-up to commit 891d44e5. Keep it limited to stop mutation key selection and regression tests.
